topic: Complex cost of living comparison with percentage increases
title: Huddersfield vs London — advanced analysis
instruction: Use the data to answer these advanced questions about cost of living.
context: A family considers moving from Huddersfield to London for a higher-paid job. Current Huddersfield costs: Rent £850 | Food £320 | Transport £55 | Utilities £140 | Childcare £900 | Other £200 | Total: £2,465/month. London equivalent costs are 81% higher overall. Current salary: £32,000/year. London job offer: £44,000/year.

questions.question: The London costs are '81% higher'. Which calculation gives the London total?
The family's disposable income is their salary minus living costs. Which sentence explains this correctly?
options: A higher salary always means more disposable income
You must compare salary increase against cost of living increase
London salaries are always worth more
Living costs do not affect disposable income
questions.answer: You must compare salary increase against cost of living increase

questions.question: What does 'net financial gain' mean when comparing two locations?
options: The total salary in each city
How much better or worse off you are financially after costs
The cost of moving between cities
questions.answer: How much better or worse off you are financially after costs

maths_section.title: Should they move? Calculate and decide.

questions.question: London monthly costs are 81% higher than Huddersfield (£2,465). What are the London monthly costs?
Huddersfield: salary £32,000/year = £2,667/month. Monthly disposable income after costs (£2,465)?
London: salary £44,000/year = £3,667/month. Monthly disposable income after London costs (£4,462)? Should they move?
